title: stevia collina
intro: Stevia collina, also known as Paraguayan Stevia, is a perennial plant native to the subtropical regions of South America, belonging to the sunflower family. The plant's leaves contain steviol glycosides, which have been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Stevia collina has been used to treat various health conditions, including high blood pressure, diabetes, and digestive issues, offering benefits such as reduced blood sugar levels and improved cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Stevia collina include teas, tinctures, and extracts, which are often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance their medicinal properties.
